subject: Math

lesson.story_mission: Join the Operations Trail team to understand division as equal sharing and equal groups.
lesson.mini_lesson: Understand division as equal sharing and equal groups.
lesson.objective: Understand division as equal sharing and equal groups.
lesson.concept_explanation: Understand division as equal sharing and equal groups. Use models, equations, and careful checking to explain each answer.
lesson.common_misconception: Learners may choose an operation too quickly or miss how the model connects to the equation.
lesson.real_world_connection: These skills help solve classroom supplies, games, snacks, teams, and sharing situations.
lesson.lesson_summary: Read the situation, build the model, write the equation, solve, and check with an inverse or another strategy.
hints: Read the problem and name what is known.
Use the model to connect numbers to an equation.
Check that the answer matches the question asked.

worked_examples.prompt: Share 12 counters equally among 3 friends. How many counters does each friend get?
worked_examples.text: Share 12 counters equally among 3 friends. How many counters does each friend get?

worked_examples.prompt: Share 15 counters equally among 5 friends. How many counters does each friend get?
worked_examples.text: Share 15 counters equally among 5 friends. How many counters does each friend get?

worked_examples.text: Share 18 counters equally among 6 friends. How many counters does each friend get?

misconception_bank.sharing_grouping_confusion: Learner may mix up number of groups with number in each group.
misconception_bank.remainder_confusion: Learner may expect leftovers in no-remainder foundation facts.
misconception_bank.division_symbol_confusion: Learner may misread the division symbol or reverse dividend and divisor.
misconception_bank.inverse_operation_confusion: Learner may not use multiplication to check division.

game_theme.title: Division Foundations
game_theme.mission_intro: Complete the Operations Trail by proving you can understand division as equal sharing and equal groups.
